decisions.summary: Extract the registration core out of projectAddCommand into a new server-safe module src/lib/project-registration.ts exporting registerProjectFromPath({path,name?}) -> {key,config}. Both the CLI (projectAddCommand) and the new POST /api/projects route consume it. No chalk/console/execSync in the helper.

Registration core may keep the existing SYNC write door (registerProjectSync) and sync hook symlinks (bounded one-shot, sanctioned by NFR-2). ONLY the `git init` subprocess must be async (execAsync/spawn) per NFR-1/PAN-70 — PAN-70 targets unbounded subprocess blocking, which only git init is here.

'new' mode ordering for no-partial-write (AC-3): derive key -> dup-check (reject before ANY fs change) -> compute target <parentDir>/<slug(name)> -> reject if target exists & non-empty -> mkdir -p -> async git init -> registerProjectFromPath. slug = name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g,'-') (matches project.ts:98).

GET /api/fs/list-dirs is HOME-CLAMPED: default start path = user's home (~); any resolved path that escapes home (via .. or an absolute path outside home) is rejected (400) and never listed. Mirror the static-route normalization in server.ts:234-246. Browsing outside home is a NonGoal for this minimal flow.

Route placement: POST /api/projects goes into the EXISTING src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts (Effect HttpRouter.add, merged in projectsRouteLayer). The folder browser GET /api/fs/list-dirs goes into a NEW src/dashboard/server/routes/fs.ts, imported + merged into makeRoutesLayer in server.ts.

Per-route auth (NOT global): POST /api/projects must call rejectUnsafeDashboardMutationRequest(request) (CSRF/mutation gate, like settings.ts:811 / issues.ts); GET /api/fs/list-dirs must call rejectUnauthorizedDashboardRequest(request) (read gate, like discovered-sessions.ts). Async exec pattern = `const execAsync = promisify(exec)` as in routes/resources.ts:29.

PAN-1969 coordination: the wiring bead implements onNewProject() to open the modal AND ensures a '+ New Project' affordance exists in the sidebar PROJECTS header + Home Projects section. If PAN-1969 already added the trigger/stub, replace the stub body and do NOT add a duplicate button. Keeps PAN-1970 independently shippable/demoable even if PAN-1969 has not merged.

Frontend modal uses the CSS-module overlay/dialog pattern matching ForkModal + command-deck.module.css (.forkHelpOverlay/.forkHelpDialog/.forkHeader/.forkFooter), not inline Tailwind. Folder picker is its own subcomponent so it is independently testable. API calls use the cookie-auth fetchWithTimeout helper (src/lib/apiFetch.ts); after success invalidate React Query keys ['command-deck-projects'] and ['registered-projects'] and select the new project (setSelectedProjectKey + usePanesStore.ensureHome).

'Add existing' accepts any folder (git repo or not) — registration does not require .git; git hooks install only when .git is present (preserves projectAddCommand behavior today). No-loss refactor: `pan project add` keeps identical resulting projects.yaml entry + console next-steps output.

hazards.summary: Cross-issue coordination with PAN-1969 (onNewProject handler + '+ New Project' button). PAN-1969 may or may not be merged when this work runs (only PRDs exist on main as of 8aabbadb68).
hazards.mitigation: Wiring bead detects an existing onNewProject/stub + trigger and reuses it; only adds the button if absent. Never render two '+ New Project' triggers.

hazards.summary: Path traversal in GET /api/fs/list-dirs — a naive readdir of an attacker-supplied ?path= could list arbitrary host directories.
hazards.mitigation: Resolve + clamp to home; reject `..` escapes and absolute paths outside home (D4). Dedicated security test is a required AC (AC-4). Reuse the normalization shape from server.ts:234-246.

hazards.summary: Partial write on failure — creating the folder / git init and THEN failing registration would leave an orphan dir or a half-registered project.
hazards.mitigation: Sequence per D3: all rejections (dup key, non-empty target) happen before any mkdir/git init. AC-3 test asserts no projects.yaml mutation on the rejection paths.

hazards.summary: git init via execSync would freeze the dashboard event loop (PAN-70).
hazards.mitigation: Use execAsync (promisify(exec)) or spawn, like routes/resources.ts. AC asserts no new execSync import in server code (NFR-1).

hazards.summary: Bypassing the single write door — writing projects.yaml directly would violate NFR-2 / single-source-of-truth.
hazards.mitigation: Register ONLY via registerProjectSync (through registerProjectFromPath). Do not add any new projects.yaml writer. AC-6.

hazards.summary: Skills <-> CLI lockstep — changing pan project add flags/help would require updating skills/pan-* in the same commit (lint-skills.sh fails CI on drift).
hazards.mitigation: The refactor must keep the projectAddCommand CLI surface (flags + help strings) identical (D9). If any help text changes, update the matching skill in the same commit.

hazards.summary: preTrustDirectory has both preTrustDirectorySync (workspace-manager.ts:1100) and preTrustDirectory (1581) exports; projectAddCommand dynamically imports preTrustDirectory.
hazards.mitigation: Helper calls the SAME symbol projectAddCommand uses today (preTrustDirectory). Verify the export at implementation time; keep the existing non-fatal try/catch around it.

sessionHistory.note: Auto-planning session for PAN-1970: minimal New Project dashboard flow (add existing / new folder + git init -> register). 8 beads across shared registration core, POST /api/projects (existing+new), GET /api/fs/list-dirs, folder picker, modal, and wiring.

feedback.markdownBody:
# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-1970

# Review Synthesis — PAN-1970 — 2026-06-19T16:22:00-04:00

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED — server-side home-directory boundary can be bypassed

## Context
- Manifest: /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970/.pan/review/agent-pan-1970-review-177f49b0/context.json
- Branch: feature/pan-1970
- Workspace: /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970
- HEAD reviewed: 177f49b0231f3a7dfb56e622978b0b2e0ec12872
- Cycle number: 1
- Prior cycle SHA: none

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Signal | Output | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| security | ready | /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970/.pan/review/agent-pan-1970-review-177f49b0/security.md | 2 |
| correctness | ready | /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970/.pan/review/agent-pan-1970-review-177f49b0/correctness.md | 1 |
| performance | ready | /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970/.pan/review/agent-pan-1970-review-177f49b0/performance.md | 0 |
| requirements | ready | /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970/.pan/review/agent-pan-1970-review-177f49b0/requirements.md | 1 |

## Blocking Findings

### [security] Symlink bypass allows browsing directories outside home — `src/dashboard/server/routes/fs.ts:35`
`GET /api/fs/list-dirs` performs the home clamp with lexical `resolve(normalize(rawPath))` string comparison against `homedir()`, then calls `readdir(target)`. A symlink inside the user's home that points outside home still passes the string-prefix check, and `readdir` follows the symlink. This violates the explicit home-clamp non-goal and can disclose directory names outside the allowed tree.

Fix by canonicalizing both the home root and requested path with `realpath` before the boundary check, rejecting missing/non-directory targets cleanly, and adding a regression test with a symlink under a temp home pointing outside that temp home.

### [security] Project registration endpoint can write to and pre-trust arbitrary absolute paths — `src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:635`
`POST /api/projects` accepts any absolute `path` for `mode='existing'` and any absolute `parentDir` for `mode='new'`, without enforcing the folder browser's home-directory boundary. A direct dashboard API caller can register, create, `git init`, and pre-trust directories outside `$HOME` wherever the server process has permission.

Fix by moving canonical home-boundary validation into shared server-side validation used by both `GET /api/fs/list-dirs` and `POST /api/projects`. Existing projects should canonicalize and require the target directory to remain under canonical `$HOME`; new projects should canonicalize the parent and final target and reject symlink escapes before filesystem writes.

### [correctness] Empty-slug project names register the parent directory under an empty key — `src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:672`
The new-project route only checks `rawName.trim()`, then derives `key = slugify(name)` and `target = join(rawParent, slugify(name))`. Names such as `!!!` pass the required-name check but slugify to `""`; `join(parentDir, "")` resolves to the parent directory, causing `git init` and registration to target the parent under an empty project key. `registerProjectFromPath` repeats the same empty-key behavior.

Fix by validating the derived slug/key before filesystem work and by rejecting empty derived keys in the shared registration core so both the dashboard route and refactored CLI cannot write invalid project registry entries.

### [requirements] New Project mode lacks the required `~/Overdeck` default parent — `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/NewProjectModal.tsx:28`
FR-4 and AC-1 require New Project mode to default the parent folder to `~/Overdeck`, preview `<parent>/<slug>`, and support the reference flow where entering `Bugs` creates `~/Overdeck/bugs`. The modal initializes `parentDir` to an empty string, renders `FolderPicker` without `initialPath`, suppresses the preview until a parent is manually selected, and disables Start while `parentDir` is empty.

Fix by initializing New Project mode with `~/Overdeck` as the concrete default parent, wiring that into the picker/preview state, and adding a modal test covering the default-parent flow.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [performance] Add a limit/pagination contract to folder browsing — `src/dashboard/server/routes/fs.ts:43`
`GET /api/fs/list-dirs` scans, filters, sorts, returns, and renders every immediate child directory. This can become slow in very large home subdirectories. Consider a server-side limit with `truncated` metadata, cursor/search pagination, or a bounded UI rendering contract.

### [performance] Avoid full-file TLDR log reads on each cost hook invocation — `sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js:30339`
`captureTldrMetricsSync()` uses byte checkpoints but still reads the whole TLDR log before slicing from the checkpoint. Long-lived workspaces with large TLDR logs will pay increasing hook cost. Consider reading only the delta from the checkpoint offset or rotating/compacting the TLDR metrics logs.

### [requirements] Stub UI manifest finding is mitigated
The manifest flagged `const [pending, setPending] = useState(false)` as suspected tab/mode state. The requirements reviewer confirmed the actual mode state is `useState<Mode>('existing')`, `switchMode()` writes it, and both exposed modes submit real `/api/projects` requests.

## Clean Sub-roles
None. All sub-roles reported either blocking or advisory findings.

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/overdeck/workspaces/feature-pan-1970/.pan/review/agent-pan-1970-review-177f49b0/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1970 -m "Fixed review issues"`

VERIFICATION FAILED for PAN-1970 (attempt 1/10):

Failed check: sync-target-branch

Sync with main FAILED — merge conflicts detected:

Merge conflicts with main:
  - .pan/continue.json
  - .pan/spec.vbrief.json
  - src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx
  - src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/NewProjectModal.tsx
  - src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/__tests__/NewProjectModal.test.tsx
  - src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx
  - src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/projects-register.test.ts
  - src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts
  - src/dashboard/server/server.ts
  - src/lib/project-registration.ts
  - sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js
  - sync-sources/hooks/record-cost-event.js.map

## REQUIRED: Resolve merge conflicts BEFORE resubmitting

1. Run: `git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main`
2. Resolve all conflicts in the listed files
3. Run the project's build and tests to verify nothing broke
4. Commit and push ALL changes

After fixing:
1. Run the project's build and tests
2. Commit and push ALL changes
3. ONLY THEN resubmit: pan review request PAN-1970 -m "Fixed sync-target-branch"

Do NOT resubmit until all repos sync cleanly and tests pass.
